parentQuestion: The parasympathetic division decreases heart rate.

scaffolds.q: The heart has its own internal pacemaker (SA node) that generates heartbeats.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The SA node fires spontaneously at about 100 beats per minute - this is the intrinsic rate without any nervous system input.

scaffolds.q: But your resting heart rate is usually around 60-70 bpm, not 100 bpm.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Something is actively SLOWING the heart below its intrinsic rate. That something is the parasympathetic system.

scaffolds.q: The vagus nerve (CN X) carries parasympathetic input to the heart.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The vagus nerve is the main parasympathetic highway to the heart. It releases acetylcholine to slow the pacemaker.

scaffolds.q: Acetylcholine (ACh) from the vagus nerve slows the SA node firing rate.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! ACh binds muscarinic receptors on pacemaker cells, opening K+ channels, hyperpolarizing the cell, slowing the firing rate.

scaffolds.q: This vagal 'brake' is always active at rest, holding heart rate below 100.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! At rest, you have 'vagal tone' - continuous parasympathetic braking that keeps your heart at 60-70 bpm instead of 100.

scaffolds.q: To increase heart rate, the parasympathetic brake is released.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! During exercise or stress, parasympathetic tone decreases AND sympathetic activity increases - both contribute to faster heart rate.

scaffolds.q: Parasympathetic = decrease heart rate; Sympathetic = increase heart rate.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This is the classic example of dual innervation with opposing effects. The actual heart rate reflects the balance between the two.

scaffolds.q: The parasympathetic division decreases heart rate via the vagus nerve.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Vagal release of ACh slows SA node firing. Your resting heart rate IS the parasympathetic in action.

scaffolds.q: The statement is TRUE - parasympathetic decreases heart rate.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is TRUE. The parasympathetic absolutely decreases heart rate - this is one of its primary effects on the cardiovascular system.
